summary: No-ship: the new env wiring only applies on fresh spawn/heal, so a live singleton `dev-env` can still keep the old or missing `GARRISON_COMPOSITION_ID` and place against the wrong composition.

findings.title: Runner leaves an already-running own-port fitting on the old composition env
findings.recommendation: Handle composition-env drift explicitly. Today `startOperativeBoundFittings()` passes `GARRISON_COMPOSITION_ID`, but if `startOwnPortFitting()` sees the fitting already running it returns `alreadyRunning` without restart, so the process keeps whatever env it booted with before. Because own-port lifecycle is keyed globally by fitting id and `up()` only tears down the same composition, starting another composition while `dev-env` is already alive still serves placements using the stale composition (or no composition). Restart or otherwise reconfigure operative-bound own-port fittings when the active composition id differs from the live instance, and add a behavioral test for the already-running case rather than only a source-invariant check.
